title: Podcast page
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Ethan Vandenberg, Justyna Acosta
description:
Zayed Law Offices would like to create a dedicated page to house the monthly uploads from Adam's podcast. Lawrank had begun this page but didn't complete it. 

What will it take to finish this page for them, and what would the timeline be? 

From the client:
Hi Ethan,
We’re looking to add a dedicated page to our website for Adam’s podcast. LawRank had started working on a layout but didn’t complete it. Here’s what they put together so far: https://zayedlawoffices.com/adam-zayed-podcast/
We’d like to move forward with a more SEO-friendly version of this page that includes embedded YouTube videos for each episode. We release a new podcast episode monthly.
Is this something your team can assist with? If so, could you provide an estimated timeframe for completion?
I appreciate your help and look forward to hearing your thoughts.

title: Main Menu Chaos Fixing
assignees: Alina Neamtu, Clayton Coombs, Mubeen Dildar-Mia
description:
https://zayedlawoffices.com/ currently has the following main menu:

About Us, with drop down:
> About Out Injury Law Firm
> Our Attorneys
> In the News
> Personal Injury Blog
> Personal Injury Resources
> Newsroom
> Attorney Referrals
> Contingency Fee
> Employment Opportunities
> Video Center

Personal Injury, with drop down:
> Birth injuries
> Car accidents
> Medical malpractice
> Motorcycle accidents
> Nursing home abuse
> Pedestrian accidents
> Truck accidents
> Wrongful death
> view all

Illinois, with drop down:
> Chicago
> Joliet
> Aurora
> Peoria
> Rockford
> Wheaton
> view all

Florida, with drop down:
> Miami
> view all

Results, with drop down
> Case results
> Client reviews

Contact us
____________________________________________________________
There are several issues that really bother me.
TL;DR: duplicate pathways, hidden clickable parent pages, overlapping content hubs, informational clutter inside the primary navigation.

1) Personal Injury links to /chicago-personal-injury-lawyers/, but it's not clickable, and that URL is already used under Illinois
Solution: replace "Personal Injury" with "Case types" and create separate case type page for Personal Injury not focused on Chicago (double check if it doesn't already exist).

2) All elements in the Personal injury drop down link to Chicago pages
Solution: create separate case type pages not focused on Chicago (double check if it doesn't already exist).

3) Illinois and Florida link to /illinois-personal-injury-lawyer/, respectively /florida-personal-injury-lawyer/, but they are not clickable.
"View all" under Illinois and Florida go to /areas-served-in-illinois/, respectively /areas-served-in-florida/. That’s unnecessary duplication.
Solution: only keep /illinois-personal-injury-lawyer/ & /florida-personal-injury-lawyer/. Copy relevant content from /areas-served-in-illinois/ and /areas-served-in-florida/ to these pages, then 301 to them.

4) "Employment Opportunities" does not belong under "About Us" in a PI conversion funnel.
Solution: move under Contact (or even footer-only?)

5) Personal Injury Blog and Personal Injury Resources have more or less the same content which created near-duplicate pages.
Solution: content pruning and only keeping the blog; move it far right since we should prioritize case types, locations, and results.

6) Video Center doesn't bring any value. It contains:
"About" video (can be added directly to the About Us page)
"Testimonials" video (can be added to Client Reviews)
"Practice Areas" videos (can be added to their respective pages)
> remove Video Center from menu and 301 to homepage

7) In the News doesn't bring any value as a stand alone page. It contains links to 15 publications and many of the articles are old (even from 12 years ago).
Solution: integrate them into the About Us page, as media logos.

8) Newsroom doesn't serve any purpose, it's just a redundant content hub with blog posts.
Solution: completely remove it from the menu and 301 to blog.

title: Content Refresh + URL Restructure

description:

title: Brand Inconsistencies Discussion
assignees: Alina Neamtu, Ethan Vandenberg, Mikey King, Mubeen Dildar-Mia
description:
Hey Team, bringing this here for us to discuss in its own thread to figure out the best path forward together.

We identified that Zayed Law Offices has inconsistent branding across their site, GBPs, and citations.

BRAND FINDINGS:
- Sites Meta Titles and Meta Descriptions mostly use "Zayed Law Offices Personal Injury Attorneys" from what I can tell.
- At the moment, we are creating citations to align with whatever the GBPs are.
- Here are the GBPs and site logo naming conventions
- Their Chicago Med Mal listing seems to match it's brand's/listing's site as its different, so no issues there


CURRENT LIST OF DBAs:
, our wonderful, amazing Bishop and brother in Christ, has confirmed with the client which DBAs they actually have. Here is that list the client gave us.

Chicago Medical Malpractice Lawyers
Chicago Truck Accident Lawyers
Miami Medical Malpractice Lawyers

We don't yet know for certain what the client's actual, legally registered entity name is; however, my educated guess is "Zayed Law Offices".

What are your guys' thoughts?

I want to clean up any NAPW inconsistencies, but I also don't want to risk GBP name updates impacting their rankings if their rankings are great, ya know? So, IDK if we update everywhere else except for any GBPs that are ranking greatly for the time being, or what.

title: Create 2 Location Pages and Update Contact Page
description:
This project should to be prioritized and completed as soon as possible.


The AM is aware that it will likely take more than 2 days to complete, but marked it as Medium Priority to emphasize it needs to be completed as soon as possible.


Message from the AM message:


" Please make it clear that this should be prioritized... I'm concerned with work for this client in particular being lost."


__________________________________________________


Domain: zayedlawoffices.com

Primary URL: https://zayedlawoffices.com/contact/


Summary:


Please create 2 new location pages for Peoria and Aurora. They should match the style of existing location pages (e.g., Rockford). The Contact page must be updated to link to them correctly.



Client Goal: Peoria and Aurora are represented as active service locations on the Contact page, each linking to a dedicated location page consistent with the existing location page format.


Requirements for Completion:

  • A dedicated location page exists for Peoria, matching the style and format of the Rockford location page
  • A dedicated location page exists for Aurora, matching the style and format of the Rockford location page
  • The Peoria location name on the Contact page links to the new Peoria location page
  • The Aurora location name on the Contact page links to the new Aurora location page


Assets and References:

Reference page (existing style): https://zayedlawoffices.com/contact/rockford-personal-injury-law-office/

Contact page with unlinked locations: https://zayedlawoffices.com/contact/


Email from the client:


"On the website,  https://zayedlawoffices.com/contact/ we have an update / 2 pages that need to be created. In the section “Call our Chicago Injury Lawyers….” In the middle of the page, you will notice a list of 7 locations, 2 of which are not hyperlinked ( Peoria and Aurora). We will need pages created for those locations in the same style as the other locations listed (Rockford, for example) and for those pages to be correctly hyperlinked. "
